data.description:
## Why

The default test suite runs serially with branch coverage — the dominant cost of the
`build-and-test` job (~22 min pytest). Measured `pytest -n4 --dist worksteal` on the same
selection is ~5× faster (1700s→~350s) with byte-identical outcomes (5877P/14S/same 1
known-Py3.14 fail), proving the suite's per-test isolation holds under parallel workers.

## What

Introduce pytest-xdist to the gating test runs, pinned to the runner's real core count.

- Add `pytest-xdist` and `pytest-split` to the `[dev]` extra in `pyproject.toml`, pinned
  exactly (both are ABSENT today; used ad hoc in experiments only).
- `gerrit-verify.yaml` + `test.yml`: change the default-suite command to
  `pytest -m "not integration and not external" -n <N> --dist worksteal …` where N is the
  runner's real vCPU count — pin `-n4` (ubuntu-latest, 4 vCPU) / `-n3` (macos arm, 3 vCPU),
  NOT `-n auto` (18-worker local runs over-subscribe and hit a coverage SQLite combine race).
- FIX COVERAGE UNDER XDIST (prereq — proven): add `parallel = true` to
  `[tool.coverage.run]` in `pyproject.toml`. Without it, `-n4 --cov` fails to combine
  worker data (reports ~37% vs the true 80%, below `fail_under=70` → red build, and leaks
  `.coverage.*` files). With `parallel = true` the combined TOTAL matches serial exactly.
- Integration tier: parallelize only NON-live tests. `pytest -m "integration and not live
  and not jira_live" -n4` is xdist-safe (64P/1S identical serial vs -n4, 1.6× faster).
  Live tests (`[integration, live]`, e.g. `test_reconcile_live_e2e.py`) share one live Jira
  project and flake under parallel load (eventual-consistency); keep them serial or confine
  to one worker via `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` + `--dist loadgroup`. In CI's integration job
  they self-skip without Jira creds, but the command must not assume that.

## Scope

- Files: `pyproject.toml` (`[dev]` deps, `[tool.coverage.run] parallel`), 
  `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, `.github/workflows/test.yml`.
- Verify runner vCPU with an in-workflow `nproc` echo (belt-and-suspenders) before pinning N.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pytest-xdist` + `pytest-split` added to `[dev]`, pinned exactly.
- [ ] `parallel = true` added to `[tool.coverage.run]`; a `-n4 --cov` run reports a combined
      TOTAL equal to the serial value and ≥ `fail_under=70`, with no `.coverage.*` files left
      in the checkout.
- [ ] gerrit-verify + test.yml default-suite step runs with `-n <real-cores> --dist worksteal`;
      test outcomes are identical to the pre-change serial run (same pass/skip counts, no new
      isolation/leak-guard failures).
- [ ] Non-live integration tier runs under `-n4`; live tests are kept serial or xdist_group-confined.
- [ ] Change passes CI (`Verified +1`) and lands via Gerrit.
data.title: Parallelize gating test runs with pytest-xdist (pinned -n, coverage-combine fix)
